codeReview.summary: The scaffolded Tauri/Vite/React baseline matches the foundation requirements in the committed repository state. I did not find a high-confidence correctness bug in the shipped scaffold itself.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature handoffs only record a single commitId, but this feature actually spans multiple commits, which makes diff-based scrutiny ambiguous.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoffs/2026-04-09T08-54-50-280Z__tauri-project-scaffold__f5a3f7d3-8469-4721-9658-03ba76a93903.json:4-6 records commitId 93e7b3a, while git history shows the scaffold landed across f690ae5 (initial commit), af64eb9 (Cargo.lock/.gitignore), and 93e7b3a (Button test only).

summary: Pass. The scaffold is usable, but the handoff metadata does not identify the full feature diff range.
